CesA1.name: Cellulose synthase A1 (primary wall)
CesA1.function: UDP-glucose → β-1,4-glucan chain; primary cell wall cellulose; rosette complex with CesA3/CesA6. Provides structural rigidity to skin cells. In treople: expressed in bark-forming keratinocytes.
CesA1.ig_note: Ħ=𐑻: β-1,4-glycosidic product; opposite chirality to animal α-1,4-glycogen

CesA4.name: Cellulose synthase A4 (secondary wall / wood)
CesA4.function: Secondary wall cellulose synthesis; rosette with CesA7/CesA8. Higher crystallinity than primary wall. In treople: structural wood-analog in long bone periosteum reinforcement and bark cortex.

COBRA.name: COBRA GPI-anchored protein
COBRA.function: Cell elongation direction control; cellulose microfibril orientation. In treople: orients cellulose deposition along growth axis of leaf-finger extensions.
COBRA.targeting: outer leaflet PM via GPI anchor

PAL1.name: Phenylalanine ammonia lyase 1
PAL1.function: Phe → trans-cinnamic acid; entry into phenylpropanoid pathway; rate-limiting step for lignin biosynthesis. In treople: expressed in cambium-like zones for bark hardening.

C4H.name: Cinnamate-4-hydroxylase (CYP73A5)
C4H.function: trans-cinnamic acid → p-coumaric acid; CYP450; ER-membrane

CAD.name: Cinnamoyl-CoA reductase / Cinnamyl alcohol dehydrogenase
CAD.function: Final steps of monolignol biosynthesis; converts aldehydes to alcohols (coniferyl, sinapyl, p-coumaryl alcohols) for lignin polymerization

LAC17.name: Laccase 17 (monolignol polymerization)
LAC17.function: Oxidative polymerization of monolignols → lignin polymer in apoplast. Requires Cu²⁺ cofactor. In treople: expressed in bark apoplast; creates structural lignified layer.
LAC17.ig_note: Ω=𐑽: lignin is a cross-linked polymer with complex winding; combined with cellulose microfibril crystalline winding gives maximum Ω

WOX4.name: WUSCHEL-related homeobox 4
WOX4.function: Vascular cambium maintenance TF; stem cell identity in lateral meristem. In treople: expressed in extremity growth zones (fingertip meristems, elbow/knee cambium rings); enables indefinite slow arboreal extension.
WOX4.ig_note: Ð=𐑺: WOX4 enables indefinite branching growth; animal centralization → arboreal expansion; Ð shift

MP_ARF5.name: MONOPTEROS / ARF5 (auxin response factor)
MP_ARF5.function: Master regulator of vascular and embryo axis formation; auxin signal → branching architecture; controls leaf-finger extension patterning

PXY.name: PHLOEM INTERCALATED WITH XYLEM receptor kinase
PXY.function: CLE41/44 receptor; controls oriented cell division in cambium; maintains xylem/phloem polarity in secondary growth zone

SPEECHLESS.name: SPEECHLESS bHLH TF (stomatal lineage initiation)
SPEECHLESS.function: Master TF for stomatal cell lineage; asymmetric entry division; expressed in meristemoid mother cells. In treople: expressed in skin basal layer to initiate stomatal lineage alongside epidermal cells.
SPEECHLESS.ig_note: Σ=𐑕: stomata enable direct CO2 uptake from air for Calvin cycle; massive increase in carbon fixation stoichiometry

MUTE.name: MUTE bHLH TF (guard mother cell fate)
MUTE.function: Symmetrical division → guard mother cell; required for guard cell pair formation

FAMA.name: FAMA bHLH TF (guard cell differentiation)
FAMA.function: Guard cell terminal differentiation; stomatal pore formation; KAT1 K+ channel expression (turgor-driven opening mechanism)

KAT1.name: K+ channel KAT1 (guard cell turgor)
KAT1.function: Inward-rectifying K+ channel; guard cell turgor control for stomatal opening/closing; light-activated via phototropin→H+-ATPase cascade

SymRK.name: Symbiosis Receptor Kinase (SymRK)
SymRK.function: LRR-RLK; receptor for mycorrhizal Myc-LCOs and rhizobial Nod factors; Ca²+ spiking initiation in symbiosis; In treople: expressed in foot sole keratinocytes/fibroblasts to enable arbuscular mycorrhizal (AM) symbiosis with soil fungi (Rhizophagus irregularis)
SymRK.ig_note: ɢ=⊙ preserved: mycorrhizal coupling = O_∞ coupling (unchanged; AM symbiosis is already O_∞ — the oldest terrestrial symbiosis)

CCaMK.name: Calcium/calmodulin-dependent protein kinase (CCaMK/DMI3)
CCaMK.function: Decodes nuclear Ca²+ oscillations from SymRK; phosphorylates CYCLOPS; required for arbuscule formation

RAM1.name: GRAS TF RAM1 (arbuscule development)
RAM1.function: GRAS domain TF; master regulator of arbuscule development; required for phosphate and lipid transfer from fungal partner; In treople: expressed in foot sole to receive P + fatty acids from Rhizophagus irregularis mycorrhiza

PT4.name: Phosphate transporter 4 (PT4/MtPT4)
PT4.function: Periarbuscular membrane H+/Pi symporter; receives phosphate from fungus; complement to carbon sent to fungus (as Glc/lipid). In treople: closes the mycorrhizal Frobenius loop — C exported to fungus, Pi imported from soil. μ∘δ=id.
PT4.ig_note: PT4 closes the mycorrhizal Frobenius: C out ⊗ Pi in = identity; the oldest terrestrial μ∘δ=id (~400 Mya)

Lhcb2.name: LHCII type-II (Lhcb2) — canopy-scale
Lhcb2.function: Additional LHCII trimer component; expanded light harvesting for canopy-density chloroplast populations in leaf-finger structures

FBPase.name: Chloroplast fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase
FBPase.function: Calvin cycle regeneration; Fru-1,6-P2 → Fru-6-P + Pi; regulated by ferredoxin/thioredoxin system; rate-limiting step in carbon fixation export

PRK.name: Phosphoribulokinase (PRK)
PRK.function: Ribulose-5-P + ATP → RuBP; regenerates RuBisCO substrate; critical for Calvin cycle flux at canopy-scale carbon fixation
